    # Plot 6: Predictions vs Ground Truth scatter (with clipping only, sample)
    try:
        fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(15, 5))
        domains = ["wikitext", "ag_news", "code"]
        domain_titles = ["WikiText", "AG News", "Code (Python)"]
        for i, (domain, title) in enumerate(zip(domains, domain_titles)):
            preds = experiment_data["with_clipping"][domain]["predictions"]
            gt = experiment_data["with_clipping"][domain]["ground_truth"]
            if len(preds) > 0 and len(gt) > 0:
                preds_flat, gt_flat = np.array(preds).flatten(), np.array(gt).flatten()
                sample_idx = np.random.choice(
                    len(preds_flat), min(2000, len(preds_flat)), replace=False
                )
                axes[i].scatter(
                    gt_flat[sample_idx], preds_flat[sample_idx], alpha=0.3, s=5
                )
                axes[i].plot([0, 1], [0, 1], "r--", label="Ideal")
                axes[i].set_xlabel("Ground Truth Importance")
                axes[i].set_ylabel("Predicted Importance")
                axes[i].set_title(f"{title}")
                axes[i].legend()
                axes[i].grid(True, alpha=0.3)
        plt.suptitle(
            "PKO Model (With Clipping): Predicted vs Ground Truth Importance Scores",
            fontsize=14,
        )
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, "pko_predictions_vs_groundtruth.png"),
            dpi=150,
            bbox_inches="tight",
        )
        plt.close()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating scatter plot: {e}")
        plt.close()

    print("All plots saved successfully!")
plot_plan:
I'll analyze the experiment data structure and create comprehensive visualizations for this gradient clipping ablation study on the PKO (Predictive Knowledge Ordering) model across three text domains.

The experiment compares training with and without gradient clipping, tracking: epoch losses (train/val), gradient norms, CDCS (Cross-Domain Consistency Score) history, and per-domain Spearman correlations for wikitext, ag_news, and code datasets. I'll create plots for: 1) Training/validation loss comparison, 2) Gradient norm evolution, 3) CDCS progression over epochs, 4) Per-domain Spearman correlation curves, 5) Final metrics comparison bar chart, and 6) Prediction vs ground truth scatter plots for each domain.

plot_analyses.analysis: Final metrics comparison (gradient clipping ablation): Across WikiText/AG News/Code, Spearman is essentially unchanged between “With Clipping” and “No Clipping” (differences are visually negligible, on the order of ~0.0–0.01). Precision and Recall are also nearly identical; if anything, “No Clipping” is marginally higher on WikiText and Code for both Precision/Recall, while AG News is effectively tied or slightly favors clipping depending on the bar. Takeaway: gradient clipping is not a key driver of final ranking quality or top-k selection quality in this setup; its effect size is much smaller than the component deltas you reported for ranking loss etc. This suggests clipping can be treated as a stability/robustness knob rather than a performance knob, and may be removed for simplicity if it doesn’t help stability in other runs/seeds.

CDCS over training (with vs without clipping): Both curves rise from ~0.74 to ~0.83+ and track closely. “No Clipping” shows slightly higher CDCS for many mid/late epochs and exhibits a noticeable spike around epoch ~14 (peaking near ~0.85) that is not mirrored by the clipped run, after which it returns to the ~0.83–0.84 band. The clipped run is a bit smoother with smaller oscillations. Takeaway: clipping mildly damps volatility but does not improve the converged CDCS; if anything, unclipped training occasionally reaches higher transient CDCS. For reporting, consider using best-of-epoch vs last-epoch: unclipped may look better on best checkpoint, while clipped may look better on stability metrics (variance across epochs/seeds).

Loss comparison over training (train/val, with vs without clipping): Train and validation losses for both conditions are almost perfectly overlapping after the initial epoch drop (from ~0.034 to ~0.019), then slowly decreasing/plateauing around ~0.018. There is no visible divergence indicating instability without clipping, nor a generalization gap difference. Takeaway: in this regime, gradients are already well-behaved (or learning rate is conservative), so clipping is effectively inactive or rarely triggered; this aligns with the minimal metric differences.

Multi-panel summary (loss, gradient norms, CDCS, and per-domain Spearman): (i) Loss curves again overlap tightly; (ii) gradient norms (measured before clipping) are extremely similar between runs, with a large initial norm (~0.049) then quickly dropping to ~0.014–0.016 and gradually decaying to ~0.008 by the end—suggesting clipping likely only matters (if at all) at the very beginning; (iii) CDCS curves match prior plot: unclipped slightly higher and more jagged, clipped slightly smoother; (iv) per-domain Spearman: WikiText converges ~0.876–0.879 with tiny differences; AG News shows more fluctuation and a notable unclipped bump around epoch ~14 (up to ~0.855) while clipped stays ~0.83–0.84; Code converges ~0.845–0.85 with small differences. Takeaway: any “benefit” of no clipping is concentrated in AG News and appears as a transient improvement; for a fair ablation, compare both last-epoch and best-epoch and include seed variance, because the effect is within typical run-to-run noise.

Predicted vs ground-truth importance scatter (with clipping) across domains: Points are heavily concentrated near low ground-truth importance (x close to 0), with predicted scores also small but showing a positive trend. The cloud sits mostly above the y=x line for very small x, indicating overestimation of tiny ground-truth importances (common when many tokens are near-zero but model outputs a small positive floor). For moderate ground-truth (x ~0.1–0.4), predictions increase but appear compressed (predicted values mostly ~0.1–0.4), suggesting limited dynamic range and potential underestimation of higher-importance tokens (few points near the top-right). Takeaway: the predictor seems to learn ordering (consistent with decent Spearman), but calibration/dynamic range is imperfect—important for proactive budget allocation because miscalibration can cause over-allocation to unimportant tokens. Consider adding a calibration loss (e.g., temperature scaling, isotonic regression offline, or a heteroscedastic regression head) or training with a tail-emphasis objective (e.g., upweight top-quantile tokens) to better separate high-importance entries.

Gradient norms over training (measured before clipping): The curves for “With Clipping” and “No Clipping” are nearly identical at all epochs, including the initial large norm and subsequent decay. This strongly suggests clipping is rarely activated or the threshold is high relative to observed norms; hence minimal impact on optimization trajectory. Takeaway: if you want clipping to matter, either increase learning rate / use a more aggressive optimizer setup, or lower the clip threshold; otherwise, you can likely omit clipping from the final method and just mention it was not impactful in ablations.

Spearman correlation by domain over training (with vs without clipping): WikiText: both conditions converge similarly; unclipped is marginally higher at the end (~0.878 vs ~0.876–0.877). AG News: more variance; unclipped shows a pronounced peak around epoch ~14 (~0.855) and ends slightly above clipped (~0.833 vs ~0.829–0.83). Code: both converge ~0.846–0.848; unclipped is slightly higher late. Takeaway: the unclipped run has a small edge in final Spearman across all domains, but the magnitude is tiny. The more important observation is domain sensitivity: AG News is noisier and benefits from checkpoint selection; this echoes your broader point that some components can flip behavior by domain. Here, clipping does not resolve that sensitivity.

vlm_feedback_summary: Received 7 plots covering a gradient clipping ablation: final per-domain metrics (Spearman/Precision/Recall), CDCS over epochs, train/val loss curves, gradient norms over epochs, per-domain Spearman over epochs, and predicted-vs-ground-truth importance scatter (with clipping). Overall, clipping has negligible effect on final metrics and loss; gradient norms are similar with/without clipping, implying clipping is rarely active. Unclipped training is slightly more volatile and occasionally reaches higher transient CDCS/Spearman (notably on AG News), while clipped training is marginally smoother.

import os
import sys

# Fix for isatty issue with redirected stdout
if not hasattr(sys.stdout, "isatty"):
    sys.stdout.isatty = lambda: False
if not hasattr(sys.stderr, "isatty"):
    sys.stderr.isatty = lambda: False

os.environ["TRANSFORMERS_NO_ADVISORY_WARNINGS"] = "1"
os.environ["TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM"] = "false"

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")
os.makedirs(working_dir, exist_ok=True)

import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
from torch.utils.data import Dataset, DataLoader
from transformers import AutoTokenizer, AutoModel, logging
from datasets import load_dataset
from scipy.stats import spearmanr
import warnings

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")
logging.set_verbosity_error()

device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
print(f"Using device: {device}")

experiment_data = {
    "no_cosine_annealing": {
        "wikitext": {
            "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "predictions": [],
            "ground_truth": [],
            "spearman": [],
        },
        "ag_news": {
            "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "predictions": [],
            "ground_truth": [],
            "spearman": [],
        },
        "code": {
            "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "predictions": [],
            "ground_truth": [],
            "spearman": [],
        },
        "cdcs_history": [],
        "epoch_losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
        "learning_rates": [],
    },
}

tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained("distilbert-base-uncased")
base_model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
    "distilbert-base-uncased", output_attentions=True
)
base_model = base_model.to(device)
base_model.eval()


def compute_importance_scores(attention_weights, seq_len):
    all_attn = torch.stack(attention_weights, dim=0)
    avg_attn = all_attn.mean(dim=(0, 2))
    importance = avg_attn.sum(dim=1)
    importance = importance / (importance.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)[0] + 1e-8)
    return importance


class ImportanceDataset(Dataset):
    def __init__(self, texts, tokenizer, base_model, max_len=128, device="cpu"):
        self.features, self.importance_scores = [], []
        base_model.eval()
        with torch.no_grad():
            for text in texts:
                if len(text.strip()) < 10:
                    continue
                encoded = tokenizer(
                    text,
                    truncation=True,
                    max_length=max_len,
                    padding="max_length",
                    return_tensors="pt",
                )
                input_ids = encoded["input_ids"].to(device)
                attention_mask = encoded["attention_mask"].to(device)
                outputs = base_model(
                    input_ids=input_ids,
                    attention_mask=attention_mask,
                    output_attentions=True,
                    output_hidden_states=True,
                )
                hidden = outputs.hidden_states[1][0].cpu()
                importance = compute_importance_scores(outputs.attentions, max_len)[
                    0
                ].cpu()
                self.features.append(hidden)
                self.importance_scores.append(importance)
                if len(self.features) >= 500:
                    break

    def __len__(self):
        return len(self.features)

    def __getitem__(self, idx):
        return self.features[idx], self.importance_scores[idx]


class PKOPredictor(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, hidden_dim=768, num_domains=3):
        super().__init__()
        self.local_conv = nn.Conv1d(hidden_dim, 128, kernel_size=3, padding=1)
        self.attention = nn.MultiheadAttention(
            hidden_dim, num_heads=4, batch_first=True
        )
        self.layer_norm = nn.LayerNorm(hidden_dim)
        self.domain_bns = nn.ModuleList(
            [nn.BatchNorm1d(hidden_dim) for _ in range(num_domains)]
        )
        self.predictor = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(hidden_dim + 128, 256),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(0.2),
            nn.Linear(256, 64),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Linear(64, 1),
        )
        self.pos_encoding = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(1, 512, hidden_dim) * 0.02)

    def forward(self, x, domain_id=0):
        batch_size, seq_len, _ = x.shape
        x = x + self.pos_encoding[:, :seq_len, :]
        attn_out, _ = self.attention(x, x, x)
        x = self.layer_norm(x + attn_out)
        x_permuted = x.permute(0, 2, 1)
        if domain_id < len(self.domain_bns):
            x_permuted = self.domain_bns[domain_id](x_permuted)
        x = x_permuted.permute(0, 2, 1)
        local_feat = F.relu(self.local_conv(x.permute(0, 2, 1))).permute(0, 2, 1)
        combined = torch.cat([x, local_feat], dim=-1)
        return torch.sigmoid(self.predictor(combined).squeeze(-1))


def ranking_loss(pred, target, margin=0.1):
    batch_size, seq_len = pred.shape
    n_pairs = min(100, seq_len * (seq_len - 1) // 2)
    idx1 = torch.randint(0, seq_len, (batch_size, n_pairs), device=pred.device)
    idx2 = torch.randint(0, seq_len, (batch_size, n_pairs), device=pred.device)
    pred1, pred2 = torch.gather(pred, 1, idx1), torch.gather(pred, 1, idx2)
    target1, target2 = torch.gather(target, 1, idx1), torch.gather(target, 1, idx2)
    return F.relu(margin - (pred1 - pred2) * torch.sign(target1 - target2)).mean()


def combined_loss(pred, target, alpha=0.5):
    return F.mse_loss(pred, target) + alpha * ranking_loss(pred, target)


def compute_metrics(pred, target):
    pred_np, target_np = (
        pred.detach().cpu().numpy().flatten(),
        target.detach().cpu().numpy().flatten(),
    )
    mask = target_np > 0.01
    if mask.sum() < 10:
        return 0.0, 0.0, 0.0
    pred_np, target_np = pred_np[mask], target_np[mask]
    spearman, _ = spearmanr(pred_np, target_np)
    spearman = 0.0 if np.isnan(spearman) else spearman
    k = max(1, len(target_np) // 10)
    top_k_pred, top_k_target = set(np.argsort(pred_np)[-k:]), set(
        np.argsort(target_np)[-k:]
    )
    precision = len(top_k_pred & top_k_target) / k
    recall = len(top_k_pred & top_k_target) / len(top_k_target) if top_k_target else 0
    return spearman, precision, recall


def compute_cdcs(spearman_scores):
    scores = np.array([s for s in spearman_scores if s > 0])
    if len(scores) == 0:
        return 0.0
    harmonic_mean = len(scores) / np.sum(1.0 / (scores + 1e-8))
    cv = min(np.std(scores) / (np.mean(scores) + 1e-8), 1.0)
    return harmonic_mean * (1 - cv)


print("Loading datasets...")
wikitext_texts = [
    t
    for t in load_dataset("wikitext", "wikitext-2-raw-v1", split="train")["text"]
    if len(t.strip()) > 50
][:1000]
ag_news_texts = [
    t for t in load_dataset("ag_news", split="train")["text"] if len(t.strip()) > 50
][:1000]
code_texts = [
    t
    for t in load_dataset("code_search_net", "python", split="train")[
        "whole_func_string"
    ]
    if t and len(t.strip()) > 50
][:1000]

print("Extracting features...")
datasets = {
    "wikitext": ImportanceDataset(wikitext_texts, tokenizer, base_model, device=device),
    "ag_news": ImportanceDataset(ag_news_texts, tokenizer, base_model, device=device),
    "code": ImportanceDataset(code_texts, tokenizer, base_model, device=device),
}

train_loaders, val_loaders = {}, {}
for name, ds in datasets.items():
    n, train_size = len(ds), int(len(ds) * 0.8)
    train_ds, val_ds = torch.utils.data.random_split(ds, [train_size, n - train_size])
    train_loaders[name] = DataLoader(train_ds, batch_size=16, shuffle=True)
    val_loaders[name] = DataLoader(val_ds, batch_size=16)

domain_to_id = {"wikitext": 0, "ag_news": 1, "code": 2}
model = PKOPredictor(hidden_dim=768, num_domains=3).to(device)
optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=1e-3, weight_decay=0.01)
# ABLATION: No cosine annealing scheduler - using constant learning rate

print("\n=== ABLATION: No Cosine Annealing (Constant LR) ===\n")

for epoch in range(20):
    model.train()
    total_train_loss, train_batches = 0, 0
    domain_iters = {d: iter(loader) for d, loader in train_loaders.items()}
    active_domains = set(domain_iters.keys())
    while active_domains:
        for domain in list(active_domains):
            try:
                features, importance = next(domain_iters[domain])
            except StopIteration:
                active_domains.discard(domain)
                continue
            features, importance = features.to(device), importance.to(device)
            optimizer.zero_grad()
            loss = combined_loss(
                model(features, domain_to_id[domain]), importance, alpha=0.3
            )
            loss.backward()
            torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 1.0)
            optimizer.step()
            total_train_loss += loss.item()
            train_batches += 1

    # ABLATION: No scheduler.step() - constant learning rate throughout training
    current_lr = optimizer.param_groups[0]["lr"]
    experiment_data["no_cosine_annealing"]["learning_rates"].append(current_lr)
    experiment_data["no_cosine_annealing"]["epoch_losses"]["train"].append(
        total_train_loss / max(train_batches, 1)
    )

    model.eval()
    total_val_loss, val_batches, domain_spearman, domain_precision = 0, 0, {}, {}
    with torch.no_grad():
        for domain, loader in val_loaders.items():
            preds, targets, domain_loss = [], [], 0
            for features, importance in loader:
                features, importance = features.to(device), importance.to(device)
                pred = model(features, domain_to_id[domain])
                domain_loss += combined_loss(pred, importance, alpha=0.3).item()
                preds.append(pred)
                targets.append(importance)
                val_batches += 1
            if preds:
                all_preds, all_targets = torch.cat(preds), torch.cat(targets)
                spearman, precision, recall = compute_metrics(all_preds, all_targets)
                domain_spearman[domain], domain_precision[domain] = spearman, precision
                experiment_data["no_cosine_annealing"][domain]["spearman"].append(
                    spearman
                )
                total_val_loss += domain_loss

    avg_val_loss = total_val_loss / max(val_batches, 1)
    experiment_data["no_cosine_annealing"]["epoch_losses"]["val"].append(avg_val_loss)
    cdcs = compute_cdcs(
        [domain_spearman.get(d, 0) for d in ["wikitext", "ag_news", "code"]]
    )
    experiment_data["no_cosine_annealing"]["cdcs_history"].append(cdcs)
    print(
        f"Epoch {epoch+1}/20: validation_loss = {avg_val_loss:.4f}, CDCS = {cdcs:.4f}, LR = {current_lr:.6f}"
    )
    print(f"  Spearman: {domain_spearman}")

final_spearman, final_precision, final_recall = {}, {}, {}
model.eval()
with torch.no_grad():
    for domain, loader in val_loaders.items():
        preds, targets = [], []
        for features, importance in loader:
            preds.append(model(features.to(device), domain_to_id[domain]))
            targets.append(importance.to(device))
        if preds:
            all_preds, all_targets = torch.cat(preds), torch.cat(targets)
            final_spearman[domain], final_precision[domain], final_recall[domain] = (
                compute_metrics(all_preds, all_targets)
            )
            experiment_data["no_cosine_annealing"][domain][
                "predictions"
            ] = all_preds.cpu().numpy()
            experiment_data["no_cosine_annealing"][domain][
                "ground_truth"
            ] = all_targets.cpu().numpy()

final_cdcs = compute_cdcs(
    [final_spearman.get(d, 0) for d in ["wikitext", "ag_news", "code"]]
)
print(f"\nFINAL CDCS (No Cosine Annealing): {final_cdcs:.4f}")
print(f"Final Spearman: {final_spearman}")

experiment_data["no_cosine_annealing"]["final_results"] = {
    "spearman": final_spearman,
    "precision": final_precision,
    "recall": final_recall,
    "cdcs": final_cdcs,
}
np.save(os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), experiment_data)

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(12, 10))
fig.suptitle("Ablation: No Cosine Annealing (Constant LR)", fontsize=14)

axes[0, 0].plot(
    experiment_data["no_cosine_annealing"]["epoch_losses"]["train"], label="Train"
)
axes[0, 0].plot(
    experiment_data["no_cosine_annealing"]["epoch_losses"]["val"], label="Val"
)
axes[0, 0].legend()
axes[0, 0].set_title("Loss")
axes[0, 0].set_xlabel("Epoch")

for domain in ["wikitext", "ag_news", "code"]:
    axes[0, 1].plot(
        experiment_data["no_cosine_annealing"][domain]["spearman"], label=domain
    )
axes[0, 1].legend()
axes[0, 1].set_title("Spearman Correlation")
axes[0, 1].set_xlabel("Epoch")

axes[1, 0].plot(experiment_data["no_cosine_annealing"]["cdcs_history"])
axes[1, 0].set_title("CDCS Over Training")
axes[1, 0].set_xlabel("Epoch")

x = np.arange(3)
axes[1, 1].bar(x, [final_spearman.get(d, 0) for d in ["wikitext", "ag_news", "code"]])
axes[1, 1].set_xticks(x)
axes[1, 1].set_xticklabels(["wikitext", "ag_news", "code"])
axes[1, 1].set_title(f"Final Spearman (CDCS={final_cdcs:.4f})")
axes[1, 1].set_ylabel("Spearman Correlation")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(os.path.join(working_dir, "pko_results.png"), dpi=150)
plt.close()

# Additional plot showing constant learning rate
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4))
ax.plot(experiment_data["no_cosine_annealing"]["learning_rates"], marker="o")
ax.set_title("Learning Rate Schedule (Constant - No Cosine Annealing)")
ax.set_xlabel("Epoch")
ax.set_ylabel("Learning Rate")
ax.set_ylim(0, 0.0015)

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

plot_plan: Looking at the experiment data, I need to create visualizations for an ablation study on cosine annealing (constant learning rate). The data contains training/validation losses, Spearman correlations for three domains (wikitext, ag_news, code), CDCS history, learning rates, and final predictions/ground truth. I'll create: 1) A training/validation loss curve, 2) Spearman correlation over epochs for each domain, 3) CDCS history plot, 4) Final Spearman comparison bar chart, 5) Learning rate schedule plot showing constant LR, and 6) Prediction vs ground truth scatter plots for each domain. Each plot will be in its own try-except block with descriptive titles mentioning the ablation study context.

plot_analyses.analysis: The learning-rate schedule is truly constant at 1e-3 across all epochs (flat line). This confirms the ablation condition “No Cosine Annealing” is implemented correctly and removes any confound from LR decay/warm restarts. Any performance change vs the cosine baseline should therefore be attributable to optimization dynamics (e.g., lack of late-stage fine-tuning) rather than LR misconfiguration.

Final per-domain Spearman correlations under constant LR are: wikitext ≈ 0.864, ag_news ≈ 0.812, code ≈ 0.823, with an aggregate CDCS reported as 0.8099. The domain ordering indicates wikitext benefits most / is easiest to rank correctly, while ag_news is the hardest under this setting. The ~0.05 gap between wikitext and ag_news suggests notable dataset-specific sensitivity to the LR schedule (or to optimization stability more generally).

CDCS over training rises sharply from a low initial value (~0.43 at epoch 0) to ~0.75 by epoch 1, then reaches ~0.83 around epochs 4–5. After that it oscillates in a narrow band (~0.78–0.84) with no clear upward trend, indicating early convergence followed by mild instability/plateau. Peaks around epochs ~13–14 (~0.84) suggest that with constant LR the model does not monotonically refine cross-domain consistency; instead it jitters around a stable region—consistent with the absence of annealing to ‘cool down’ updates late in training.

Predictions-vs-ground-truth scatter plots show a strong concentration of ground-truth values near 0 with predicted values also near 0–0.2, plus a small number of higher-ground-truth points. Across domains, the predictor appears compressed in range (rarely predicting above ~0.3–0.45 except for an outlier near (1,1)), implying underestimation of high-importance tokens and limited calibration at the top end. Despite that, rank correlation can remain high if relative ordering among the many low-importance items is preserved; however, this compression likely hurts top-k retrieval metrics and any use-case requiring accurate identification of rare high-importance tokens (needles). Domain-wise, ag_news shows slightly more spread into mid-range ground truth (~0.2–0.4) and predictions track the diagonal somewhat better there, but still with noticeable underprediction at higher values.

Joint training curves: (i) Loss drops steeply in the first 1–2 epochs (train ~0.041→~0.021; val ~0.028→~0.019) and then flattens around ~0.0183–0.0188 with minimal train–val gap, suggesting little overfitting and that optimization quickly reaches a basin. (ii) Spearman by epoch quickly jumps to ~0.82–0.86 by epoch 1–3 and then stays stable; wikitext remains highest and most stable (~0.86–0.87), while ag_news and code fluctuate more (~0.80–0.85). (iii) CDCS curve mirrors this: rapid early gains then a plateau with small oscillations. Overall, constant LR yields fast early learning but lacks late-stage smoothing; cosine annealing would typically reduce these oscillations and may yield slightly better final CDCS if the baseline shows higher stability/peak.

Per-domain Spearman trajectories highlight different stability profiles: wikitext rapidly reaches ~0.84 by epoch 1 and then stays in a tight band (~0.85–0.87), indicating robust learning under constant LR. ag_news shows larger variance, including a notable dip around epoch ~15 (down to ~0.80) after reaching ~0.86 earlier, implying sensitivity to constant-step updates and potential benefit from annealing or early stopping. code ramps more gradually and stabilizes around ~0.82–0.84, with moderate oscillations. This pattern suggests the LR schedule interacts with domain difficulty/label noise: harder/noisier domains (ag_news) benefit more from annealing to prevent late-epoch degradation.

This is a duplicate visualization of the constant learning-rate schedule (flat at 1e-3). It reinforces that no decay is applied and that training length (20 epochs) is not accompanied by any LR reduction, which can explain the observed late-epoch metric jitter and occasional regressions (especially on ag_news).

Training/validation loss under constant LR shows a classic ‘fast drop then plateau’ behavior, with train and val nearly overlapping after epoch ~2. Minor bumps in val loss around epochs ~7 and ~14–16 coincide with periods where ag_news Spearman dips, consistent with constant LR causing small optimization overshoots. The absence of widening train–val gap suggests generalization is not the main issue; rather, the schedule may be preventing fine-grained convergence to a better ranking/calibration solution.

vlm_feedback_summary: Received 8 plots for the “No Cosine Annealing (Constant LR=1e-3)” ablation: constant LR schedule, final per-domain Spearman bar chart with CDCS=0.8099, CDCS-over-epochs curve, predictions-vs-ground-truth scatter (3 domains), combined panel of loss/Spearman/CDCS/final bars, per-domain Spearman-over-epochs, and train/val loss-over-epochs. Overall: rapid early convergence, stable but slightly jittery plateau; wikitext strongest, ag_news weakest/most unstable; scatter suggests prediction range compression/underestimation of high-importance tokens.

overall_plan:
Ablation name: No Dropout Regularization.
The ablation study removes the dropout layer (0.2 dropout rate) from the PKOPredictor's predictor network to examine its role in preventing overfitting and improving cross-domain generalization. The key modification is in the `PKOPredictor` class where the `nn.Dropout(0.2)` layer is removed from the `self.predictor` sequential block. The experiment will track training/validation losses, Spearman correlations, and CDCS metrics to assess whether removing dropout leads to overfitting or degraded cross-domain performance. Results are saved and visualized with appropriate labels indicating this is the "No Dropout" ablation.

import os
import sys

# Fix for isatty issue with redirected stdout
if not hasattr(sys.stdout, "isatty"):
    sys.stdout.isatty = lambda: False
if not hasattr(sys.stderr, "isatty"):
    sys.stderr.isatty = lambda: False

os.environ["TRANSFORMERS_NO_ADVISORY_WARNINGS"] = "1"
os.environ["TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM"] = "false"

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")
os.makedirs(working_dir, exist_ok=True)

import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
from torch.utils.data import Dataset, DataLoader
from transformers import AutoTokenizer, AutoModel, logging
from datasets import load_dataset
from scipy.stats import spearmanr
import warnings

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")
logging.set_verbosity_error()

device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
print(f"Using device: {device}")

experiment_data = {
    "no_dropout_ablation": {
        "wikitext": {
            "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "predictions": [],
            "ground_truth": [],
            "spearman": [],
        },
        "ag_news": {
            "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "predictions": [],
            "ground_truth": [],
            "spearman": [],
        },
        "code": {
            "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "predictions": [],
            "ground_truth": [],
            "spearman": [],
        },
        "cdcs_history": [],
        "epoch_losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
    }
}

tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained("distilbert-base-uncased")
base_model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
    "distilbert-base-uncased", output_attentions=True
)
base_model = base_model.to(device)
base_model.eval()


def compute_importance_scores(attention_weights, seq_len):
    all_attn = torch.stack(attention_weights, dim=0)
    avg_attn = all_attn.mean(dim=(0, 2))
    importance = avg_attn.sum(dim=1)
    importance = importance / (importance.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)[0] + 1e-8)
    return importance


class ImportanceDataset(Dataset):
    def __init__(self, texts, tokenizer, base_model, max_len=128, device="cpu"):
        self.features, self.importance_scores = [], []
        base_model.eval()
        with torch.no_grad():
            for text in texts:
                if len(text.strip()) < 10:
                    continue
                encoded = tokenizer(
                    text,
                    truncation=True,
                    max_length=max_len,
                    padding="max_length",
                    return_tensors="pt",
                )
                input_ids = encoded["input_ids"].to(device)
                attention_mask = encoded["attention_mask"].to(device)
                outputs = base_model(
                    input_ids=input_ids,
                    attention_mask=attention_mask,
                    output_attentions=True,
                    output_hidden_states=True,
                )
                hidden = outputs.hidden_states[1][0].cpu()
                importance = compute_importance_scores(outputs.attentions, max_len)[
                    0
                ].cpu()
                self.features.append(hidden)
                self.importance_scores.append(importance)
                if len(self.features) >= 500:
                    break

    def __len__(self):
        return len(self.features)

    def __getitem__(self, idx):
        return self.features[idx], self.importance_scores[idx]


class PKOPredictorNoDropout(nn.Module):
    """PKOPredictor with dropout removed from the predictor network (ablation study)"""

    def __init__(self, hidden_dim=768, num_domains=3):
        super().__init__()
        self.local_conv = nn.Conv1d(hidden_dim, 128, kernel_size=3, padding=1)
        self.attention = nn.MultiheadAttention(
            hidden_dim, num_heads=4, batch_first=True
        )
        self.layer_norm = nn.LayerNorm(hidden_dim)
        self.domain_bns = nn.ModuleList(
            [nn.BatchNorm1d(hidden_dim) for _ in range(num_domains)]
        )
        # ABLATION: Removed nn.Dropout(0.2) from the predictor network
        self.predictor = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(hidden_dim + 128, 256),
            nn.ReLU(),
            # Dropout layer removed here (was: nn.Dropout(0.2))
            nn.Linear(256, 64),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Linear(64, 1),
        )
        self.pos_encoding = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(1, 512, hidden_dim) * 0.02)

    def forward(self, x, domain_id=0):
        batch_size, seq_len, _ = x.shape
        x = x + self.pos_encoding[:, :seq_len, :]
        attn_out, _ = self.attention(x, x, x)
        x = self.layer_norm(x + attn_out)
        x_permuted = x.permute(0, 2, 1)
        if domain_id < len(self.domain_bns):
            x_permuted = self.domain_bns[domain_id](x_permuted)
        x = x_permuted.permute(0, 2, 1)
        local_feat = F.relu(self.local_conv(x.permute(0, 2, 1))).permute(0, 2, 1)
        combined = torch.cat([x, local_feat], dim=-1)
        return torch.sigmoid(self.predictor(combined).squeeze(-1))


def ranking_loss(pred, target, margin=0.1):
    batch_size, seq_len = pred.shape
    n_pairs = min(100, seq_len * (seq_len - 1) // 2)
    idx1 = torch.randint(0, seq_len, (batch_size, n_pairs), device=pred.device)
    idx2 = torch.randint(0, seq_len, (batch_size, n_pairs), device=pred.device)
    pred1, pred2 = torch.gather(pred, 1, idx1), torch.gather(pred, 1, idx2)
    target1, target2 = torch.gather(target, 1, idx1), torch.gather(target, 1, idx2)
    return F.relu(margin - (pred1 - pred2) * torch.sign(target1 - target2)).mean()


def combined_loss(pred, target, alpha=0.5):
    return F.mse_loss(pred, target) + alpha * ranking_loss(pred, target)


def compute_metrics(pred, target):
    pred_np, target_np = (
        pred.detach().cpu().numpy().flatten(),
        target.detach().cpu().numpy().flatten(),
    )
    mask = target_np > 0.01
    if mask.sum() < 10:
        return 0.0, 0.0, 0.0
    pred_np, target_np = pred_np[mask], target_np[mask]
    spearman, _ = spearmanr(pred_np, target_np)
    spearman = 0.0 if np.isnan(spearman) else spearman
    k = max(1, len(target_np) // 10)
    top_k_pred, top_k_target = set(np.argsort(pred_np)[-k:]), set(
        np.argsort(target_np)[-k:]
    )
    precision = len(top_k_pred & top_k_target) / k
    recall = len(top_k_pred & top_k_target) / len(top_k_target) if top_k_target else 0
    return spearman, precision, recall


def compute_cdcs(spearman_scores):
    scores = np.array([s for s in spearman_scores if s > 0])
    if len(scores) == 0:
        return 0.0
    harmonic_mean = len(scores) / np.sum(1.0 / (scores + 1e-8))
    cv = min(np.std(scores) / (np.mean(scores) + 1e-8), 1.0)
    return harmonic_mean * (1 - cv)


print("Loading datasets...")
wikitext_texts = [
    t
    for t in load_dataset("wikitext", "wikitext-2-raw-v1", split="train")["text"]
    if len(t.strip()) > 50
][:1000]
ag_news_texts = [
    t for t in load_dataset("ag_news", split="train")["text"] if len(t.strip()) > 50
][:1000]
code_texts = [
    t
    for t in load_dataset("code_search_net", "python", split="train")[
        "whole_func_string"
    ]
    if t and len(t.strip()) > 50
][:1000]

print("Extracting features...")
datasets = {
    "wikitext": ImportanceDataset(wikitext_texts, tokenizer, base_model, device=device),
    "ag_news": ImportanceDataset(ag_news_texts, tokenizer, base_model, device=device),
    "code": ImportanceDataset(code_texts, tokenizer, base_model, device=device),
}

train_loaders, val_loaders = {}, {}
for name, ds in datasets.items():
    n, train_size = len(ds), int(len(ds) * 0.8)
    train_ds, val_ds = torch.utils.data.random_split(ds, [train_size, n - train_size])
    train_loaders[name] = DataLoader(train_ds, batch_size=16, shuffle=True)
    val_loaders[name] = DataLoader(val_ds, batch_size=16)

domain_to_id = {"wikitext": 0, "ag_news": 1, "code": 2}
ablation_data = experiment_data["no_dropout_ablation"]

print("\n" + "=" * 60)
print("ABLATION STUDY: No Dropout Regularization")
print("=" * 60)
print("Removed nn.Dropout(0.2) from PKOPredictor's predictor network")
print("=" * 60 + "\n")

model = PKOPredictorNoDropout(hidden_dim=768, num_domains=3).to(device)
optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=1e-3, weight_decay=0.01)
scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=20)

for epoch in range(20):
    model.train()
    total_train_loss, train_batches = 0, 0
    domain_iters = {d: iter(loader) for d, loader in train_loaders.items()}
    active_domains = set(domain_iters.keys())
    while active_domains:
        for domain in list(active_domains):
            try:
                features, importance = next(domain_iters[domain])
            except StopIteration:
                active_domains.discard(domain)
                continue
            features, importance = features.to(device), importance.to(device)
            optimizer.zero_grad()
            loss = combined_loss(
                model(features, domain_to_id[domain]), importance, alpha=0.3
            )
            loss.backward()
            torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 1.0)
            optimizer.step()
            total_train_loss += loss.item()
            train_batches += 1
    scheduler.step()
    ablation_data["epoch_losses"]["train"].append(
        total_train_loss / max(train_batches, 1)
    )

    model.eval()
    total_val_loss, val_batches, domain_spearman, domain_precision = 0, 0, {}, {}
    with torch.no_grad():
        for domain, loader in val_loaders.items():
            preds, targets, domain_loss = [], [], 0
            for features, importance in loader:
                features, importance = features.to(device), importance.to(device)
                pred = model(features, domain_to_id[domain])
                domain_loss += combined_loss(pred, importance, alpha=0.3).item()
                preds.append(pred)
                targets.append(importance)
                val_batches += 1
            if preds:
                all_preds, all_targets = torch.cat(preds), torch.cat(targets)
                spearman, precision, recall = compute_metrics(all_preds, all_targets)
                domain_spearman[domain], domain_precision[domain] = spearman, precision
                ablation_data[domain]["spearman"].append(spearman)
                total_val_loss += domain_loss

    avg_val_loss = total_val_loss / max(val_batches, 1)
    ablation_data["epoch_losses"]["val"].append(avg_val_loss)
    cdcs = compute_cdcs(
        [domain_spearman.get(d, 0) for d in ["wikitext", "ag_news", "code"]]
    )
    ablation_data["cdcs_history"].append(cdcs)
    print(
        f"Epoch {epoch+1}/20: train_loss = {ablation_data['epoch_losses']['train'][-1]:.4f}, val_loss = {avg_val_loss:.4f}, CDCS = {cdcs:.4f}"
    )
    print(f"  Spearman: {domain_spearman}")

final_spearman, final_precision, final_recall = {}, {}, {}
model.eval()
with torch.no_grad():
    for domain, loader in val_loaders.items():
        preds, targets = [], []
        for features, importance in loader:
            preds.append(model(features.to(device), domain_to_id[domain]))
            targets.append(importance.to(device))
        if preds:
            all_preds, all_targets = torch.cat(preds), torch.cat(targets)
            final_spearman[domain], final_precision[domain], final_recall[domain] = (
                compute_metrics(all_preds, all_targets)
            )
            ablation_data[domain]["predictions"] = all_preds.cpu().numpy()
            ablation_data[domain]["ground_truth"] = all_targets.cpu().numpy()

final_cdcs = compute_cdcs(
    [final_spearman.get(d, 0) for d in ["wikitext", "ag_news", "code"]]
)
print(f"\n{'='*60}")
print(f"NO DROPOUT ABLATION RESULTS")
print(f"{'='*60}")
print(f"FINAL CDCS: {final_cdcs:.4f}")
print(f"Final Spearman: {final_spearman}")
print(f"Final Precision: {final_precision}")
print(f"Final Recall: {final_recall}")

ablation_data["final_results"] = {
    "spearman": final_spearman,
    "precision": final_precision,
    "recall": final_recall,
    "cdcs": final_cdcs,
}

np.save(os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), experiment_data)

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(12, 10))
fig.suptitle("No Dropout Ablation Study", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold")

axes[0, 0].plot(ablation_data["epoch_losses"]["train"], label="Train", color="blue")
axes[0, 0].plot(ablation_data["epoch_losses"]["val"], label="Val", color="orange")
axes[0, 0].legend()
axes[0, 0].set_xlabel("Epoch")
axes[0, 0].set_ylabel("Loss")
axes[0, 0].set_title("Training vs Validation Loss (No Dropout)")

for domain in ["wikitext", "ag_news", "code"]:
    axes[0, 1].plot(ablation_data[domain]["spearman"], label=domain)
axes[0, 1].legend()
axes[0, 1].set_xlabel("Epoch")
axes[0, 1].set_ylabel("Spearman Correlation")
axes[0, 1].set_title("Spearman Correlation per Domain")

axes[1, 0].plot(ablation_data["cdcs_history"], color="green", linewidth=2)
axes[1, 0].set_xlabel("Epoch")
axes[1, 0].set_ylabel("CDCS")
axes[1, 0].set_title("Cross-Domain Consistency Score (CDCS)")

x = np.arange(3)
bars = axes[1, 1].bar(
    x,
    [final_spearman.get(d, 0) for d in ["wikitext", "ag_news", "code"]],
    color=["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c"],
)
axes[1, 1].set_xticks(x)
axes[1, 1].set_xticklabels(["wikitext", "ag_news", "code"])
axes[1, 1].set_ylabel("Spearman Correlation")
axes[1, 1].set_title(f"Final Results (CDCS={final_cdcs:.4f})")
for i, bar in enumerate(bars):
    height = bar.get_height()
    axes[1, 1].text(
        bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2.0,
        height,
        f"{height:.3f}",
        ha="center",
        va="bottom",
        fontsize=10,
    )

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(os.path.join(working_dir, "pko_results_no_dropout.png"), dpi=150)
plt.close()

print(f"\nResults saved to {working_dir}")
print("Done!")

plot_plan: I'll analyze the experiment data structure and create visualizations for the No Dropout ablation study. The data contains training/validation losses, Spearman correlations per domain (wikitext, ag_news, code), CDCS history, and final predictions vs ground truth. I'll create separate plots for: (1) training vs validation loss curves, (2) Spearman correlation progression per domain, (3) CDCS history over epochs, (4) final Spearman comparison bar chart, and (5) scatter plots showing predictions vs ground truth for each domain. Each plot will be in its own try-except block and saved with descriptive names.

plot_analyses.analysis: Training vs validation loss (no-dropout ablation): both curves drop sharply from ~0.032 at epoch 0 to ~0.019 by epoch 1, then slowly decrease and plateau. The train loss continues a gentle downward trend to ~0.0175 by the final epoch, while validation stabilizes slightly higher (~0.0179–0.0181) with small oscillations. The persistent but small generalization gap (~3e-4 to 6e-4) suggests mild overfitting but generally stable optimization; removing dropout does not cause divergence or a widening gap in this run. The early rapid improvement implies the predictor learns the coarse importance mapping quickly; later epochs yield diminishing returns, so early stopping around ~10–12 epochs might capture most gains while reducing training time.

Spearman correlation per domain over epochs (no dropout): wikitext rises quickly from ~0.76 to ~0.85+ by epoch 1–2 and then gradually improves/plateaus around ~0.87–0.88, indicating strong and stable rank-order predictability in this domain. code increases from ~0.76 to ~0.80+ early and then stabilizes around ~0.82–0.83 with modest fluctuations, suggesting decent but lower predictability than wikitext. ag_news starts much lower (~0.63) and climbs to ~0.75 by epoch 1, then oscillates more (notably a dip around epoch ~8) and ends around ~0.79–0.80, indicating higher variance and potentially noisier/less consistent importance signals. Overall, no-dropout training yields stable correlations, but domain sensitivity is clear: wikitext > code > ag_news in Spearman.

Final metrics per domain (no dropout): reported final CDCS = 0.7949. Spearman: wikitext 0.877, ag_news 0.791, code 0.828. Precision@10%: wikitext 0.702, ag_news 0.741, code 0.611. This highlights a key trade-off: higher Spearman does not necessarily imply higher top-k precision (ag_news has lower Spearman than wikitext but higher P@10). code shows relatively strong Spearman yet the lowest P@10, suggesting calibration/score distribution issues (good global ranking but weaker separation at the extreme top), or that the evaluation’s “top 10%” set is harder to nail in code due to many near-ties/long-tail importance.

Predictions vs ground truth scatter (no dropout) across domains: points are heavily concentrated near low ground-truth importance (x close to 0), consistent with a sparse-importance label distribution (most tokens unimportant). Predicted importances occupy a narrow band (mostly <~0.2–0.25), indicating compression of the dynamic range and underestimation of high-importance tokens. In wikitext and code, many points lie above the y=x line at very small x (overpredicting small importances), while for moderate x (e.g., 0.2–0.4) predictions often fall below the ideal line (underpredicting), implying miscalibration: the model inflates low-importance tokens and fails to assign sufficiently high scores to truly important ones. ag_news shows a somewhat broader spread up to ~0.4–0.45 predicted for moderate ground truth, but still exhibits saturation. This pattern can explain why Spearman can be decent (relative ordering among low-importance tokens) while Precision@10% suffers (insufficient separation for the top tail).

Composite dashboard (no dropout): consolidates the same signals—loss curves stable with small gap; Spearman trajectories show fast early gains and plateau; CDCS climbs from ~0.65 to ~0.79–0.80 with a notable mid-training dip around epoch ~8 before recovering, suggesting either learning-rate/optimization noise or domain-batch effects that temporarily reduce cross-domain consistency. The final bar chart reiterates domain ranking (wikitext best Spearman, code second, ag_news third) and overall CDCS=0.7949. The mid-epoch CDCS dip without a corresponding catastrophic loss spike hints that CDCS is sensitive to small domain-specific ranking shifts; this is a useful diagnostic for interaction effects in future ablations.

CDCS over epochs (no dropout): CDCS increases sharply from ~0.65 at epoch 0 to ~0.75 at epoch 1 and ~0.77–0.78 by epoch 2–3, then gradually improves toward ~0.79–0.80. There is a pronounced drop around epoch ~8 (to ~0.758) followed by recovery and eventual peak around ~0.80–0.802 near epoch ~17, then slight settling ~0.795. This indicates that cross-domain generalization is mostly learned early, but is somewhat unstable mid-training; regularization (e.g., dropout) might smooth this trajectory even if the final CDCS is similar. Given the plateau, the no-dropout configuration likely benefits from either early stopping or adding a calibration-focused auxiliary loss to better preserve tail separation rather than training longer.

vlm_feedback_summary: Received 6 plots for a no-dropout ablation of the PKO predictor: (1) train vs val loss, (2) Spearman per domain over epochs, (3) final Spearman & Precision@10% per domain with CDCS=0.7949, (4) prediction-vs-ground-truth scatter for three domains, (5) a combined dashboard repeating these, and (6) CDCS over epochs. Overall: stable optimization with small generalization gap; strong Spearman in wikitext, moderate in code, more variable in ag_news; CDCS ~0.795 with a mid-training dip; scatter suggests dynamic-range compression/miscalibration that can hurt top-k precision, especially in code.

import os
import sys

# Fix for isatty issue with redirected stdout
if not hasattr(sys.stdout, "isatty"):
    sys.stdout.isatty = lambda: False

os.environ["TRANSFORMERS_NO_ADVISORY_WARNINGS"] = "1"

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")

import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
from torch.utils.data import Dataset, DataLoader
from transformers import AutoTokenizer, AutoModel, logging
from datasets import load_dataset
from scipy.stats import spearmanr
import warnings

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")
logging.set_verbosity_error()

device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
print(f"Using device: {device}")

experiment_data = {
    "with_layernorm": {
        "wikitext": {
            "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "predictions": [],
            "ground_truth": [],
            "spearman": [],
        },
        "ag_news": {
            "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "predictions": [],
            "ground_truth": [],
            "spearman": [],
        },
        "code": {
            "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "predictions": [],
            "ground_truth": [],
            "spearman": [],
        },
        "cdcs_history": [],
        "epoch_losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
        "final_results": {},
    },
    "no_layernorm": {
        "wikitext": {
            "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "predictions": [],
            "ground_truth": [],
            "spearman": [],
        },
        "ag_news": {
            "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "predictions": [],
            "ground_truth": [],
            "spearman": [],
        },
        "code": {
            "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "predictions": [],
            "ground_truth": [],
            "spearman": [],
        },
        "cdcs_history": [],
        "epoch_losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
        "final_results": {},
    },
}

tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained("distilbert-base-uncased")
base_model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
    "distilbert-base-uncased", output_attentions=True
)
base_model = base_model.to(device)
base_model.eval()


def compute_importance_scores(attention_weights, seq_len):
    all_attn = torch.stack(attention_weights, dim=0)
    avg_attn = all_attn.mean(dim=(0, 2))
    importance = avg_attn.sum(dim=1)
    importance = importance / (importance.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)[0] + 1e-8)
    return importance


class ImportanceDataset(Dataset):
    def __init__(self, texts, tokenizer, base_model, max_len=128, device="cpu"):
        self.features, self.importance_scores = [], []
        base_model.eval()
        with torch.no_grad():
            for text in texts:
                if len(text.strip()) < 10:
                    continue
                encoded = tokenizer(
                    text,
                    truncation=True,
                    max_length=max_len,
                    padding="max_length",
                    return_tensors="pt",
                )
                input_ids = encoded["input_ids"].to(device)
                attention_mask = encoded["attention_mask"].to(device)
                outputs = base_model(
                    input_ids=input_ids,
                    attention_mask=attention_mask,
                    output_attentions=True,
                    output_hidden_states=True,
                )
                hidden = outputs.hidden_states[1][0].cpu()
                importance = compute_importance_scores(outputs.attentions, max_len)[
                    0
                ].cpu()
                self.features.append(hidden)
                self.importance_scores.append(importance)
                if len(self.features) >= 500:
                    break

    def __len__(self):
        return len(self.features)

    def __getitem__(self, idx):
        return self.features[idx], self.importance_scores[idx]


class PKOPredictor(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, hidden_dim=768, num_domains=3, use_layernorm=True):
        super().__init__()
        self.use_layernorm = use_layernorm
        self.local_conv = nn.Conv1d(hidden_dim, 128, kernel_size=3, padding=1)
        self.attention = nn.MultiheadAttention(
            hidden_dim, num_heads=4, batch_first=True
        )
        if use_layernorm:
            self.layer_norm = nn.LayerNorm(hidden_dim)
        self.domain_bns = nn.ModuleList(
            [nn.BatchNorm1d(hidden_dim) for _ in range(num_domains)]
        )
        self.predictor = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(hidden_dim + 128, 256),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(0.2),
            nn.Linear(256, 64),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Linear(64, 1),
        )
        self.pos_encoding = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(1, 512, hidden_dim) * 0.02)

    def forward(self, x, domain_id=0):
        batch_size, seq_len, _ = x.shape
        x = x + self.pos_encoding[:, :seq_len, :]
        attn_out, _ = self.attention(x, x, x)
        if self.use_layernorm:
            x = self.layer_norm(x + attn_out)
        else:
            x = x + attn_out  # Skip layer normalization
        x_permuted = x.permute(0, 2, 1)
        if domain_id < len(self.domain_bns):
            x_permuted = self.domain_bns[domain_id](x_permuted)
        x = x_permuted.permute(0, 2, 1)
        local_feat = F.relu(self.local_conv(x.permute(0, 2, 1))).permute(0, 2, 1)
        combined = torch.cat([x, local_feat], dim=-1)
        return torch.sigmoid(self.predictor(combined).squeeze(-1))


def ranking_loss(pred, target, margin=0.1):
    batch_size, seq_len = pred.shape
    n_pairs = min(100, seq_len * (seq_len - 1) // 2)
    idx1 = torch.randint(0, seq_len, (batch_size, n_pairs), device=pred.device)
    idx2 = torch.randint(0, seq_len, (batch_size, n_pairs), device=pred.device)
    pred1, pred2 = torch.gather(pred, 1, idx1), torch.gather(pred, 1, idx2)
    target1, target2 = torch.gather(target, 1, idx1), torch.gather(target, 1, idx2)
    return F.relu(margin - (pred1 - pred2) * torch.sign(target1 - target2)).mean()


def combined_loss(pred, target, alpha=0.5):
    return F.mse_loss(pred, target) + alpha * ranking_loss(pred, target)


def compute_metrics(pred, target):
    pred_np, target_np = (
        pred.detach().cpu().numpy().flatten(),
        target.detach().cpu().numpy().flatten(),
    )
    mask = target_np > 0.01
    if mask.sum() < 10:
        return 0.0, 0.0, 0.0
    pred_np, target_np = pred_np[mask], target_np[mask]
    spearman, _ = spearmanr(pred_np, target_np)
    spearman = 0.0 if np.isnan(spearman) else spearman
    k = max(1, len(target_np) // 10)
    top_k_pred, top_k_target = set(np.argsort(pred_np)[-k:]), set(
        np.argsort(target_np)[-k:]
    )
    precision = len(top_k_pred & top_k_target) / k
    recall = len(top_k_pred & top_k_target) / len(top_k_target) if top_k_target else 0
    return spearman, precision, recall


def compute_cdcs(spearman_scores):
    scores = np.array([s for s in spearman_scores if s > 0])
    if len(scores) == 0:
        return 0.0
    harmonic_mean = len(scores) / np.sum(1.0 / (scores + 1e-8))
    cv = min(np.std(scores) / (np.mean(scores) + 1e-8), 1.0)
    return harmonic_mean * (1 - cv)


print("Loading datasets...")
wikitext_texts = [
    t
    for t in load_dataset("wikitext", "wikitext-2-raw-v1", split="train")["text"]
    if len(t.strip()) > 50
][:1000]
ag_news_texts = [
    t for t in load_dataset("ag_news", split="train")["text"] if len(t.strip()) > 50
][:1000]
code_texts = [
    t
    for t in load_dataset("code_search_net", "python", split="train")[
        "whole_func_string"
    ]
    if t and len(t.strip()) > 50
][:1000]

print("Extracting features...")
datasets = {
    "wikitext": ImportanceDataset(wikitext_texts, tokenizer, base_model, device=device),
    "ag_news": ImportanceDataset(ag_news_texts, tokenizer, base_model, device=device),
    "code": ImportanceDataset(code_texts, tokenizer, base_model, device=device),
}

train_loaders, val_loaders = {}, {}
for name, ds in datasets.items():
    n, train_size = len(ds), int(len(ds) * 0.8)
    train_ds, val_ds = torch.utils.data.random_split(
        ds, [train_size, n - train_size], generator=torch.Generator().manual_seed(42)
    )
    train_loaders[name] = DataLoader(train_ds, batch_size=16, shuffle=True)
    val_loaders[name] = DataLoader(val_ds, batch_size=16)

domain_to_id = {"wikitext": 0, "ag_news": 1, "code": 2}


def train_and_evaluate(use_layernorm, config_name):
    print(f"\n{'='*50}")
    print(f"Training configuration: {config_name} (use_layernorm={use_layernorm})")
    print(f"{'='*50}")

    torch.manual_seed(42)
    model = PKOPredictor(hidden_dim=768, num_domains=3, use_layernorm=use_layernorm).to(
        device
    )
    optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=1e-3, weight_decay=0.01)
    scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=20)

    data = experiment_data[config_name]

    for epoch in range(20):
        model.train()
        total_train_loss, train_batches = 0, 0
        domain_iters = {d: iter(loader) for d, loader in train_loaders.items()}
        active_domains = set(domain_iters.keys())
        while active_domains:
            for domain in list(active_domains):
                try:
                    features, importance = next(domain_iters[domain])
                except StopIteration:
                    active_domains.discard(domain)
                    continue
                features, importance = features.to(device), importance.to(device)
                optimizer.zero_grad()
                loss = combined_loss(
                    model(features, domain_to_id[domain]), importance, alpha=0.3
                )
                loss.backward()
                torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 1.0)
                optimizer.step()
                total_train_loss += loss.item()
                train_batches += 1
        scheduler.step()
        data["epoch_losses"]["train"].append(total_train_loss / max(train_batches, 1))

        model.eval()
        total_val_loss, val_batches, domain_spearman = 0, 0, {}
        with torch.no_grad():
            for domain, loader in val_loaders.items():
                preds, targets, domain_loss = [], [], 0
                for features, importance in loader:
                    features, importance = features.to(device), importance.to(device)
                    pred = model(features, domain_to_id[domain])
                    domain_loss += combined_loss(pred, importance, alpha=0.3).item()
                    preds.append(pred)
                    targets.append(importance)
                    val_batches += 1
                if preds:
                    all_preds, all_targets = torch.cat(preds), torch.cat(targets)
                    spearman, precision, recall = compute_metrics(
                        all_preds, all_targets
                    )
                    domain_spearman[domain] = spearman
                    data[domain]["spearman"].append(spearman)
                    total_val_loss += domain_loss

        avg_val_loss = total_val_loss / max(val_batches, 1)
        data["epoch_losses"]["val"].append(avg_val_loss)
        cdcs = compute_cdcs(
            [domain_spearman.get(d, 0) for d in ["wikitext", "ag_news", "code"]]
        )
        data["cdcs_history"].append(cdcs)
        print(
            f"Epoch {epoch+1}/20: val_loss = {avg_val_loss:.4f}, CDCS = {cdcs:.4f}, Spearman: {domain_spearman}"
        )

    # Final evaluation
    final_spearman, final_precision, final_recall = {}, {}, {}
    model.eval()
    with torch.no_grad():
        for domain, loader in val_loaders.items():
            preds, targets = [], []
            for features, importance in loader:
                preds.append(model(features.to(device), domain_to_id[domain]))
                targets.append(importance.to(device))
            if preds:
                all_preds, all_targets = torch.cat(preds), torch.cat(targets)
                (
                    final_spearman[domain],
                    final_precision[domain],
                    final_recall[domain],
                ) = compute_metrics(all_preds, all_targets)
                data[domain]["predictions"] = all_preds.cpu().numpy().tolist()
                data[domain]["ground_truth"] = all_targets.cpu().numpy().tolist()

    final_cdcs = compute_cdcs(
        [final_spearman.get(d, 0) for d in ["wikitext", "ag_news", "code"]]
    )
    data["final_results"] = {
        "spearman": final_spearman,
        "precision": final_precision,
        "recall": final_recall,
        "cdcs": final_cdcs,
    }

    print(f"\n{config_name} - FINAL CDCS: {final_cdcs:.4f}")
    print(f"{config_name} - Final Spearman: {final_spearman}")
    return final_cdcs, final_spearman


# Run ablation study
cdcs_with_ln, spearman_with_ln = train_and_evaluate(
    use_layernorm=True, config_name="with_layernorm"
)
cdcs_no_ln, spearman_no_ln = train_and_evaluate(
    use_layernorm=False, config_name="no_layernorm"
)

# Summary
print("\n" + "=" * 60)
print("ABLATION STUDY SUMMARY: Layer Normalization Impact")
print("=" * 60)
print(f"With LayerNorm    - CDCS: {cdcs_with_ln:.4f}, Spearman: {spearman_with_ln}")
print(f"Without LayerNorm - CDCS: {cdcs_no_ln:.4f}, Spearman: {spearman_no_ln}")
print(f"CDCS Difference: {cdcs_with_ln - cdcs_no_ln:.4f}")

np.save(os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), experiment_data)

# Plotting
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(15, 10))

# Training Loss comparison
axes[0, 0].plot(
    experiment_data["with_layernorm"]["epoch_losses"]["train"],
    label="With LN - Train",
    linestyle="-",
)
axes[0, 0].plot(
    experiment_data["with_layernorm"]["epoch_losses"]["val"],
    label="With LN - Val",
    linestyle="-",
)
axes[0, 0].plot(
    experiment_data["no_layernorm"]["epoch_losses"]["train"],
    label="No LN - Train",
    linestyle="--",
)
axes[0, 0].plot(
    experiment_data["no_layernorm"]["epoch_losses"]["val"],
    label="No LN - Val",
    linestyle="--",
)
axes[0, 0].legend()
axes[0, 0].set_title("Loss Comparison")
axes[0, 0].set_xlabel("Epoch")

# CDCS comparison
axes[0, 1].plot(
    experiment_data["with_layernorm"]["cdcs_history"], label="With LayerNorm"
)
axes[0, 1].plot(experiment_data["no_layernorm"]["cdcs_history"], label="No LayerNorm")
axes[0, 1].legend()
axes[0, 1].set_title("CDCS Over Training")
axes[0, 1].set_xlabel("Epoch")

# Final CDCS bar chart
x = np.arange(2)
axes[0, 2].bar(x, [cdcs_with_ln, cdcs_no_ln], color=["blue", "orange"])
axes[0, 2].set_xticks(x)
axes[0, 2].set_xticklabels(["With LN", "No LN"])
axes[0, 2].set_title("Final CDCS Comparison")
axes[0, 2].set_ylabel("CDCS")

# Per-domain Spearman comparison
for idx, domain in enumerate(["wikitext", "ag_news", "code"]):
    axes[1, idx].plot(
        experiment_data["with_layernorm"][domain]["spearman"], label="With LayerNorm"
    )
    axes[1, idx].plot(
        experiment_data["no_layernorm"][domain]["spearman"], label="No LayerNorm"
    )
    axes[1, idx].legend()
    axes[1, idx].set_title(f"{domain} Spearman")
    axes[1, idx].set_xlabel("Epoch")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(os.path.join(working_dir, "pko_results.png"), dpi=150)
plt.close()

print("\nAblation study complete! Results saved.")

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")

try:
    experiment_data = np.load(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), allow_pickle=True
    ).item()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error loading experiment data: {e}")
    experiment_data = None

# Plot 1: Training/Validation Loss Comparison
try:
    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
    plt.plot(
        experiment_data["with_layernorm"]["epoch_losses"]["train"],
        label="With LN - Train",
        linestyle="-",
        color="blue",
    )
    plt.plot(
        experiment_data["with_layernorm"]["epoch_losses"]["val"],
        label="With LN - Val",
        linestyle="--",
        color="blue",
    )
    plt.plot(
        experiment_data["no_layernorm"]["epoch_losses"]["train"],
        label="No LN - Train",
        linestyle="-",
        color="orange",
    )
    plt.plot(
        experiment_data["no_layernorm"]["epoch_losses"]["val"],
        label="No LN - Val",
        linestyle="--",
        color="orange",
    )
    plt.xlabel("Epoch")
    plt.ylabel("Loss")
    plt.title(
        "PKO Layer Normalization Ablation Study\nTraining and Validation Loss Comparison"
    )
    plt.legend()
    plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

        os.path.join(working_dir, "pko_layernorm_ablation_loss_curves.png"),
        dpi=150,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error creating loss plot: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 2: CDCS Over Training
try:
    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
    plt.plot(
        experiment_data["with_layernorm"]["cdcs_history"],
        label="With LayerNorm",
        marker="o",
        markersize=3,

    )
    plt.xlabel("Epoch")
    plt.ylabel("CDCS")

        "PKO Layer Normalization Ablation Study\nCross-Domain Consistency Score (CDCS) Over Training"
    )
    plt.legend()
    plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "pko_layernorm_ablation_cdcs_history.png"),
        dpi=150,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error creating CDCS history plot: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 3: Final Results Bar Chart
try:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 5))
    final_with = experiment_data["with_layernorm"]["final_results"]
    final_no = experiment_data["no_layernorm"]["final_results"]

    # CDCS comparison
    axes[0].bar(
        ["With LayerNorm", "No LayerNorm"],
        [final_with["cdcs"], final_no["cdcs"]],
        color=["blue", "orange"],
    )
    axes[0].set_ylabel("CDCS")
    axes[0].set_title("Final CDCS Comparison")

    # Spearman per domain
    domains = ["wikitext", "ag_news", "code"]
    x = np.arange(len(domains))
    width = 0.35
    axes[1].bar(
        x - width / 2,
        [final_with["spearman"].get(d, 0) for d in domains],

    plt.suptitle("PKO Layer Normalization Ablation Study - Final Results", fontsize=12)

        dpi=150,

except Exception as e:

# Plot 4: Per-Domain Spearman Over Epochs
try:

    domains = ["wikitext", "ag_news", "code"]

except Exception as e:

# Plot 5: Predictions vs Ground Truth Scatter

    domains = ["wikitext", "ag_news", "code"]

            axes[row, col].set_title(f"{config_name} - {domain}")

    plt.suptitle(

except Exception as e:

plot_plan:
I'll create visualizations for this ablation study comparing PKO (Prior Knowledge Optimization) with and without Layer Normalization across three datasets (wikitext, ag_news, code). The experiment data contains training/validation losses, CDCS (Cross-Domain Consistency Score) history, per-domain Spearman correlations, and final predictions vs ground truth.

My plotting plan:
1. Training and validation loss curves comparing both configurations
2. CDCS progression over epochs for both configurations
3. Final CDCS and Spearman bar chart comparison
4. Per-domain Spearman correlation over epochs (3 subplots)
5. Prediction vs Ground Truth scatter plots for final validation samples

plot_analyses.analysis: CDCS over training (With LayerNorm vs No LayerNorm): Both conditions converge to very similar CDCS (~0.82–0.83) by the end of training, indicating LayerNorm is not a major driver of final cross-domain consistency in this setup. The main difference is early training stability: With LayerNorm starts very low at epoch 0 (~0.57) then rapidly jumps to ~0.77 by epoch 1, whereas No LayerNorm starts much higher (~0.76) and improves more smoothly. After ~epoch 3, curves largely overlap with small oscillations; occasional mid-training dips (e.g., With LN around epoch ~7) recover quickly. Takeaway: LN mainly affects optimization dynamics/initialization sensitivity rather than asymptotic CDCS; if compute budget is tight, you could shorten warmup/early epochs differently per setting, but you shouldn’t expect meaningful final CDCS gains from LN alone.

Predictions vs Ground Truth scatter (top row With LN; bottom row No LN) across wikitext/ag_news/code: In all domains, points are heavily concentrated near low ground-truth importance (x close to 0), reflecting a sparse-importance label distribution (most tokens low importance). Both With LN and No LN show a systematic bias: predictions are often higher than ground truth for very small x (cloud above the y=x line near the origin), suggesting overestimation of low-importance tokens (potentially reducing achievable compression). For moderate ground-truth values (~0.2–0.5), alignment with the diagonal improves, especially in ag_news where a visible band tracks y≈x. Differences between With LN and No LN are subtle: wikitext appears slightly tighter/less dispersed with LN at moderate x; code looks similar, with both showing limited coverage of high-importance region (few points beyond x>0.3). Takeaway: LN does not drastically change calibration; the dominant issue is class imbalance/sparsity and overprediction near zero—consider loss reweighting, quantile regression, or explicitly optimizing top-k ranking/recall of high-importance tokens rather than MSE-style calibration.

Training/validation loss curves (With LN vs No LN): Loss drops sharply in the first epoch for both settings, then plateaus around ~0.0178–0.0185 with very small train–val gaps, indicating little overfitting and that both models reach similar minima. No LN starts with slightly higher initial train loss but reaches the same plateau quickly. The near-identical late-epoch losses corroborate the CDCS similarity: LN is not providing a clear generalization advantage here. Takeaway: if LN adds overhead/complexity, removing it likely won’t hurt final objective; however, the different early-epoch behavior suggests LN interacts with learning rate schedule/initialization—ensure fair tuning (e.g., same LR may not be optimal for both).

Multi-panel summary (loss comparison, CDCS over training, final CDCS bar, per-domain Spearman over training): Final CDCS bars are essentially equal (~0.83 with LN vs ~0.82–0.83 without), reinforcing that LN contributes marginally at best. Per-domain Spearman: wikitext ends slightly higher with LN (~0.875 vs ~0.872), ag_news is nearly identical (~0.82 both), and code ends slightly higher with LN (~0.846 vs ~0.844). Over training, No LN sometimes starts higher in ag_news (epoch 0) but LN catches up quickly; wikitext/code show LN improving faster from a worse start. Takeaway: LN provides a small, consistent edge in rank-correlation for wikitext/code but negligible for ag_news; effect sizes look within noise—needs multi-seed CIs to claim significance. Practical recommendation: keep LN if you care about maximum correlation robustness across domains; drop it if you prioritize simplicity/latency and can tolerate tiny correlation changes.

Final results bars (Final CDCS and final Spearman by domain): The final CDCS difference is minimal (With LN slightly higher by a few thousandths). Final Spearman by domain shows similarly tiny deltas: wikitext marginally favors LN; ag_news is effectively tied; code marginally favors LN. Takeaway: LN is a low-importance component relative to others (e.g., your ranking loss effect sizes); it likely won’t explain major cross-domain consistency improvements. For the component hierarchy, LN should be ranked as ‘minor’ unless multi-seed testing reveals consistent, statistically significant gains.

Spearman correlation per domain over training (separate figure): Wikitext: With LN starts much lower then rapidly rises and slightly surpasses No LN after early epochs, ending around ~0.875. AG_NEWS: No LN starts high (~0.84) while With LN starts very low (~0.55) then catches up by ~epoch 2–3; thereafter both fluctuate around ~0.80–0.83 with frequent crossings—suggesting high variance and weak dependence on LN. CODE: With LN again starts lower but overtakes and stays slightly above No LN for much of training, ending ~0.846–0.848. Takeaway: LN mainly changes early optimization trajectory; once trained, domain-wise rank correlation is dominated by other factors. The frequent crossings in ag_news imply either higher noise in labels/metric or insufficient batch size—consider reporting smoothed curves and running more seeds.

vlm_feedback_summary: Received multiple plots for a LayerNorm ablation in PKO: CDCS vs epoch, prediction-vs-ground-truth scatter across domains, loss curves, and per-domain Spearman trajectories plus final bar summaries. Overall, LayerNorm has negligible impact on final CDCS and only tiny, likely non-significant improvements in final Spearman for wikitext/code; its main effect is on early training dynamics. Scatter plots suggest a broader calibration issue (overpredicting near-zero importance) that LN does not fix.

overall_plan:
Ablation name: No Multi-Domain Round-Robin Training.
The ablation study compares the baseline round-robin training strategy (interleaving batches from different domains) against sequential domain training (completing all batches from one domain before moving to the next). I'll modify the training loop to implement sequential training where we iterate through each domain completely before moving to the next, while keeping everything else identical. The experiment will track metrics for both training strategies and compare their final CDCS scores and per-domain Spearman correlations to assess whether interleaved domain mixing is important for cross-domain generalization.

metric_names.description: Training loss during model training
Validation loss during model training
Cross-Domain Correlation Score measuring overall model performance
Spearman correlation coefficient for ROUND_ROBIN training strategy
Spearman correlation coefficient for SEQUENTIAL training strategy
Top-K Precision for ROUND_ROBIN training strategy
Top-K Recall for ROUND_ROBIN training strategy
Top-K Precision for SEQUENTIAL training strategy
Top-K Recall for SEQUENTIAL training strategy
